_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-32R Magnesium Sulfate (MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-32R: Magnesium Sulfate — MDHHS Reviewed 2025
indications: Severe asthma — status asthmaticus not responding to bronchodilators
Cardiac arrest — Torsades de Pointes (polymorphic VT)
Symptomatic Torsades de Pointes with pulse
contraindications: Severe renal failure (may accumulate — relative contraindication in life-threatening emergencies)
precautions: MUST be diluted and infused over ~10 minutes — rapid IV push can cause cardiac arrest, respiratory arrest, or severe hypotension
Monitor: respiratory rate (respiratory depression is early toxicity sign), BP, deep tendon reflexes (loss precedes respiratory depression), mental status
Calcium gluconate or calcium chloride is the antidote for magnesium toxicity
Burning sensation at IV site is common and expected
expected_effects: Cardiac membrane stabilization — termination of Torsades de Pointes
notes: Dilute in 100-250 mL NS and infuse over ~10 minutes for all indications
Eclamptic seizure / severe pre-eclampsia: 4 grams over 10 minutes
Cardiac arrest (Torsades): 2 grams IV/IO rapid infusion (may give faster in arrest)
Monitor respiratory rate continuously — rate <10/min is sign of toxicity

dosing.dose: 2 grams diluted in 100-250 mL NS; infuse over ~10 minutes
dosing.notes: For bronchospasm not responding to albuterol and ipratropium; monitor RR and BP

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — eclamptic seizure / severe pre-eclampsia
dosing.dose: 4 grams diluted in 100-250 mL NS; infuse over ~10 minutes
dosing.notes: Definitive hospital treatment follows — prehospital dose is loading dose

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — cardiac arrest (Torsades de Pointes)
dosing.notes: May administer more rapidly in cardiac arrest (pulseless); dilute when possible

used_in_protocols: Tachycardia — Torsades de Pointes with Pulse
